evals.expected_output: Claude asks for site type, tech stack, and hosting platform, then offers an audit profile selection (quick, full, local, ecommerce, custom). After gathering context it fetches the URL HTML, runs module auto-detection, and begins the core audit (SEO, Security, Accessibility, Performance) producing a prioritised findings list with P0-P3 ratings.

evals.prompt: Audit https://shopify-store.example.com — it's a Shopify e-commerce site on Shopify hosting
evals.expected_output: Claude detects e-commerce signals (Product schema, cart elements, add-to-cart buttons) from the HTML and auto-enables the E-commerce module alongside core modules. Findings include product schema validation, payment trust badges, breadcrumb checks, and price/availability markup. The output explicitly lists 'E-commerce module: auto-detected'.

evals.prompt: Audit https://joes-plumbing.example.com — it's a local plumbing business website built with WordPress
evals.expected_output: Claude detects local business signals (LocalBusiness schema, Google Maps embed, tel: links) and auto-enables the Local SEO module. Findings include NAP consistency check, Google Business Profile verification, service area markup, local trust signals, and call-to-action placement. Output explicitly lists 'Local SEO module: auto-detected'.

evals.prompt: Audit https://multilingual.example.com — the site has hreflang tags and a language switcher
evals.expected_output: Claude detects i18n signals (hreflang tags, language switcher elements) and auto-enables the i18n module. Findings include hreflang validation, x-default presence, RTL support check, and lang attribute verification. Output explicitly lists 'i18n module: auto-detected'.

evals.prompt: Crawl https://example.com to depth 2 and audit all discovered pages
evals.expected_output: Claude runs scripts/crawl.py with --url https://example.com --depth 2, respecting robots.txt and same-domain filtering. It then runs the audit pipeline on each discovered page and produces a consolidated findings list across all crawled URLs, noting which findings appear on which pages.

evals.prompt: Audit these sites in bulk: [{"url": "https://site-a.com", "name": "Site A"}, {"url": "https://site-b.com", "name": "Site B"}]
evals.expected_output: Claude writes the site list to a JSON file, then runs scripts/bulk_audit.py with --sites and --profile quick. Output includes a comparison table showing each site's overall score, grade (A-F), and per-category scores side by side.

evals.prompt: Run the CI gate on this scores file with threshold 70 and fail on P0 findings. The scores.json has overall_score 85 and no P0 findings.
evals.expected_output: Claude runs scripts/ci_gate.py --scores scores.json --threshold 70 --fail-on P0. Since overall_score (85) exceeds threshold (70) and there are no P0 findings, the script exits with code 0. Claude reports: 'CI gate passed: score 85 >= 70, no P0 findings detected.'

evals.prompt: Run the CI gate on this scores file with threshold 80 and fail on P0 findings. The scores.json has overall_score 65 and 2 P0 findings.
evals.expected_output: Claude runs scripts/ci_gate.py --scores scores.json --threshold 80 --fail-on P0. The script exits with code 1 (score 65 < threshold 80) or code 2 (P0 findings detected). Claude reports: 'CI gate failed: score 65 is below threshold 80, and 2 P0 findings were detected.' Lists the specific P0 findings.

evals.prompt: Audit https://example.com and give me the report in client-facing mode — my client is non-technical
evals.expected_output: Claude runs the audit with --client-facing flag. Output replaces technical jargon: 'HSTS' becomes 'browser security header', 'CSP' becomes 'content security policy', 'P0 Critical' becomes 'Urgent', 'P1 High' becomes 'Important'. Code blocks are stripped from fix suggestions. The HTML dashboard uses plain-English labels throughout.

evals.prompt: Compare my site https://mysite.com with competitor https://competitor.com
evals.expected_output: Claude runs the full audit on both URLs, then presents a side-by-side score comparison table showing overall score, grade, and per-category scores (SEO, Security, Accessibility, Performance) for both sites. Includes actionable suggestions listing which specific checks the competitor passes that mysite.com fails, prioritised by impact.
